name: Neuro On-Call Quick References

Seizure Medications & Dosing

Anti-seizure medication reference table, benzodiazepine dosing, and weight-based guides

Generic [Trade]Abbrev (Yr)FDA‑Labeled IndicationFormulation (§ = generic)Cost†Mechanism of ActionAdult Dose (mg/day) initial → maxChild Dose initial ; maint mg/kg/dayTypical Dose increases Time ➜ Steady‑State / PKT½ (h), % bound [ther. level μg/mL]Key Drug InteractionsCommon / Serious Adverse EffectsNotes & PearlsMonitoring / “To Do”
Acetazolamide [Diamox]AZM (1952)Adjunct for various seizure types (off-label)Tab, IV$Carbonic anhydrase inhibitor250 → 1000 (BID-TID)5 ; 10–15↑ 250 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 5 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)2–3 d; T½ 6–9 h, 90 %May alter excretion of other drugs (e.g., lithium)Paresthesia, fatigue / metabolic acidosis, nephrolithiasisTolerance may develop; not first-line; catamenial epilepsyElectrolytes/renal funtion, CBC
Brivaracetam [Briviact]BRV (2016)Adj. focalTabs, soln, IV$$High‑affinity SV2A50 → 2001 ; 2–5 (BID)↑ 25–50 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 1 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)2 d; T½ 9 h, 20 %Minor CYP2C19Somnolence, dizzinessFaster IV push vs LEVLFT in hepatic dz
Cannabidiol [Epidiolex]CBD (2018)Dravet, LGS, TSCOral soln 100 mg/mL$$$ECS / TRPV1 modulator100 → 700 mg (wt‑based)5 ; 10–20 (max 25) BID↑ 2.5–5 mg/kg/d q1–2wk2–4 wk; T½ 56–61 h, 94 % [—]↑ CLB, ↑ everolimus (CYP2C19/3A)Diarrhea, ↓ appetite / ↑LFTWeight‑based; hold ≥ 3× ULN ALTLFT q month × 3, then q 3–6 m
Carbamazepine [Tegretol]CBZ (1968)Focal, trigeminal neuralgiaTabs, susp§$Na⁺ block200 → 16005 ; 10–30 (BID)↑ 200 mg/d qwk (ad); ↑ 2–5 mg/kg/d qwk (p)Auto‑induction 3 wk; T½ 12–17 h, 80 % [4–12]Strong inducer; ↓ OCP, CLZDiplopia, rash / aplastic anemia, SJS/TENHLA‑B*15:02 in AsiansCBC, Na, LFT q 6 m
Cenobamate [Xcopri]CNB (2019)Focal (adult)Tabs$$Na⁺ late‑block, GABA-A modulator50 → 400— (not FDA peds)↑ 50 mg q2wk (ad; not FDA peds)4 wk; T½ 50–60 h, 60 % Induce CYP3ASomnolence, DRESSOff‑label trials ≥12 yTitrate slowly; rash watch
Clobazam (Onfi)CLB (2011)LGS (≥2 y)Tabs, susp 2.5 mg/1mL$$GABA-A modulator10 → 400.25; 0.5-1.0 (divided BID)↑ 5–10 mg/d qwk (ad); ↑ 0.1–0.2 mg/kg/d qwk (p)SS 5–9 d; T½ 36–42 h; ~85% [0.1–0.4]CYP3A4/2C19 sub; ↑ by CBD/flucon.; ↓ by CBZ/PHT/PBSomn., drooling, behav. change / resp. dep., SJS, WD szSch. IV; tolerance—consider drug holidays; taper slow; off‑label: focalSedation/behavior; LFT if hepatic dz; caution w/ opioids
Clonazepam [Klonopin]CLZ (1975)Lennox-Gastaut, absence, myoclonicTab, ODT$GABA-A modulator0.5 → 20 (div BID-TID)0.01 ; 0.05–0.1 (div BID-TID)↑ 0.25–0.5 mg q3d (ad); ↑ 0.01–0.03 mg/kg/d q3d (p)3–5 d; T½ 30–40 h, 85 %Additive CNS depression with other ASMsSedation, ataxia / dependence, respiratory depressionTaper slowly to avoid withdrawal seizuresMonitor sedation, respiratory status in polytherapy
Eslicarbazepine [Aptiom]ESL (2013)Focal epilepsy (adjunct/monotherapy)Tab$$Prolongs Na⁺ inactivation400 → 1200 QD10 ; 20–30 (div QD)↑ 400–600 mg qwk (ad); ↑ 5–10 mg/kg/d qwk (p)4–5 d; T½ 20–24 h, 40 %↓ by strong enzyme inducersDizziness, hyponatremia / rash, DRESSOnce-daily dosing; monitor Na⁺BMP baseline & periodic (Na⁺)
Ethosuximide [Zarontin]ETX (1958)CAECaps, susp§$T‑Ca²⁺ block500 → 150010 ; 20–40 (BID)↑ 250 mg q4–7d (ad); ↑ 5 mg/kg/d q4–7d (p)1 wk; T½ 30–60 h, <10 % [40–100]↓ by EIAEDsGI upset, hiccups / SJSTake with food/full H₂OCBC, LFT q 6 m
Felbamate [Felbatol]FBM (1993)LGS, refractory focalTabs, susp$$NMDA antag, GABA↑1200 → 360015 ; 30–60 (TID)↑ 600 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 15 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)1–2 wk; T½ 20 h, 25 %Strong inducer & inhibInsomnia / aplastic anemia, hepatic failSigned consent requiredCBC, LFT monthly
Fenfluramine [Fintepla]FFA (2020)Dravet, LGSOral soln 2.2 mg/mL$$$5‑HT2B/2C agonist2 → 26 mg0.2 ; 0.35–0.7Varies – see full dosing guidelines1–2 wk; T½ 20–30 h, 50 % [—]SSRIs/SNRIs ⇒ 5‑HT syndrome↓Appetite, somnolence / valvulopathy, QTDispense via REMS (echo, EKG)Echo & EKG baseline + q 6 m
Gabapentin [Neurontin]GBP (1993)Adj. focal, painCaps, soln§$α2δ‑Ca²⁺ mod900 → 360010 ; 30–60 (TID)↑ 300 mg q1–3d (ad); ↑ 10 mg/kg/d q3d (p)1 d; T½ 5–7 h, 0 %↓ bioavail w/ antacidsSedation, wt gainNon‑linear abs; taper offRenal dose adjust
Ganaxolone [Ztalmy]GNX (2022)CDKL5 deficiencyOral susp 50 mg/mL$$$Neurosteroid GABA‑A positive allosteric modulator600 → 1800 mg7 ; 21 TIDVaries – see full dosing guidelines7 d; T½ 34 h, 99 % [—]Substrate CYP3A4Somnolence, fever / status szAdminister with foodLFTs baseline & periodic
Lacosamide [Vimpat]LCM (2009)Adj. focalTabs, soln, IV$$Slow Na⁺ inactivation100 → 4001 ; 5–10 (BID)↑ 50 mg qwk (ad); ↑ 1–2 mg/kg/d qwk (p)3 d; T½ 13 h, <15 %MinimalDizziness, PR↑ / AF rareScreen ECG if cardiac hxECG if on PR‑prolongers

Levetiracetam [Keppra]LEV (1999)Broad incl. JMETabs, soln, IV§$$SV2A binder500 → 300010 ; 20–60 (BID)↑ 500 mg q2wk (ad); ↑ 10 mg/kg/d q2wk (p)2 d; T½ 6–8 h, <10 % [—]Nil clinicallyIrritability, somnolencePyridoxine may curb mood AEMood screen, behavior q visit
Methsuximide [Celontin]MSX (1952)Refractory absence seizuresCap$$T-type Ca²⁺ channel blocker300 → 1200 (div BID)10 ; 20–25Varies – see full dosing guidelines3–7 d; T½ 60 h, 0–50 %May ↑ phenytoin; additive CNS effectsGI upset, drowsiness / blood dyscrasias, hepatotoxicityReserved for refractory casesCBC, LFTs regularly
Oxcarbazepine [Trileptal]OXC (2000)Focal, 2° GTCSusp, tabs§$Na⁺ block300 → 24005 ; 20–50 (BID)↑ 300 mg q3–7d (ad); ↑ 5–10 mg/kg/d qwk (p)3 d; T½ MHD 9 h, <40 %↓ OCP; cross‑rxn CBZHyponatremia, rashCheck Na⁺ esp elderlyNa⁺ q 6–12 m
Perampanel [Fycompa]PER (2012)Focal, GTCTabs, susp$$Non‑comp AMPA antag2 → 12 mg QHS(≥4 y) 2 ; 4–8↑ 2 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 0.04 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)2–3 wk; T½ 105 h, 95 %↓ by EIAEDsSedation, aggression / SUDEP signalBlack‑box: homicidal ideationPsych screen q visit
Phenobarbital [Luminal]PB (1912)Neonatal, GTCElixir, tabs§$GABA‑A ↑, Na⁺ block60 → 3003 ; 5–10 (QD)↑ 30–60 mg qwk (ad); ↑ 1–3 mg/kg/d qwk (p)Instant; T½ 75–120 h, 50 % [15–40]Strong inducerSedation / cognitive decline, boneOnce‑daily dosing helps adherenceVit D + bone screen
Phenytoin [Dilantin]PHT (1938)Focal, SECaps, susp§, IV$Na⁺ block (zero‑order)300 → 6005 ; 5–10 (QD)↑ 100 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 2–5 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)5 d; T½ 22 h, 90 % [10–20]Inducer; ↓ OCP, warfarinGingival hyperplasia / DRESSAvoid generic switchesLevel q 6–12 m
Pregabalin [Lyrica]PGB (2004)Adj.*, pain, anxietyCaps, soln$$α2δ‑Ca²⁺ mod150 → 6002 ; 2–10 (BID)↑ 75 mg q1wk (ad); ↑ 1–2 mg/kg/d q1wk (p)2 d; T½ 6 h, 0 %MinimalEdema, wt gain / abuseNot common in pedsWeight, mood
Primidone [Mysoline]PRM (1953)Focal, tremorTabs§$Pro‑drug → PB, PEMA250 → 20005 ; 15 (TID)↑ 125 mg q3d (ad); ↑ 5–10 mg/kg/d q3d (p)2–3 wk; PB T½ 120 hInducer (as PB)Sedation, depression / osteoporosisRarely used nowBone health, levels
Rufinamide [Banzel]RUF (2008)LGSTab §; oral susp$$Prolongs Na⁺ inactivation400 → 320010 ; 45–50 (div BID)↑ 400–800 mg q2d (ad); ↑ 10 mg/kg/d q2d (p)2–3 d; T½ 6–10 h, 34 %↓ by CBZ/PB; ↑ by VPAQTshort, GI upset / DRESSGive w/ food; screen QTECG baseline if cardiac hx
Stiripentol [Diacomit]STP (2018 US)Dravet adj.Cap / powder§$$$GABA potentiation (CYP inhib)1000 → 3000 mg15 ; 50–100 (BID‑TID)↑ 250 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 5 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)3–5 d; T½ 4–13 h, 99 %↑ CLB, VPA, PBN/V, neutropenia / somnolenceMust co‑admin with CLB ± VPACBC, LFT q 3 m
Tiagabine [Gabitril]TGB (1997)Adj. focalTabs$GAT‑1 reuptake inhib4 → 560.1 ; 1–2 (BID)↑ 4–8 mg qwk (ad); ↑ 0.05 mg/kg/d qwk (p)2 d; T½ 7–9 h, 96 %↓ by EIAEDs; ↑ VPASedation / NCSE in non‑EAvoid off‑label anxietyCounsel on dusk dosing
Topiramate [Topamax]TPM (1996)LGS, IS, migraineTabs, sprinkles, ER$–$$↑ GABA, Na⁺/Ca²⁺ block, AMPA25 → 4001 ; 5–9 (BID)↑ 25–50 mg q1–2wk (ad); ↑ 0.5–1 mg/kg/d q1–2wk (p)4 wk; T½ 21 h, 15 % [2–25]↓ OCP, ↑ by EIAEDsParesthesias, wt loss / nephrolithiasis, anhidrosisEncourage fluids; monitor growthHCO3 baseline & periodic
Valproate [Depakote]VPA (1978)Broad, migraineTabs, sprinkles, IV§$–$$↑ GABA, Na⁺ block250 → 300010 ; 20–60 (BID)↑ 5–10 mg/kg/d qwk (ad/p)2–4 d; T½ 5–15 h, 90 % [50–100]Enz inhib; ↑ LTG, PBWt gain, tremor / hepatotox, NTDsHigh teratogenicityCBC, LFT (q6m); ammonia
Vigabatrin [Sabril]VGB (2009 US)IS, focalPackets§$$Irrev GABA‑T inhib500 → 400025 ; 50–150 (BID)↑ 500 mg qwk (ad); ↑ 25–50 mg/kg/wk (p)2 d; T½ 5 h, 0 %NoneWeight gain, drowsy / PVFDREMS: field perimetryVision (Baseline + q6m)

Febrile Seizure On-Call References

Quick reference guides for febrile seizure evaluation and management

🚨

Pediatric LVO Escalation Pathway

Emergency escalation for suspected pediatric large vessel occlusion requiring endovascular intervention

Step 1 — Call Anesthesia

  • Age <12 years: Call Peds anesthesia — ask if they have availability / can go to NCMC
  • Age >12 years: Call adult anesthesia at NCMC — ask if they would take the patient
  • If yes → Call endovascular neurosurgery
  • If no → Proceed to Step 2

Step 2 — Outside Hospitals Transfer Center — Call Multiple

Call multiple simultaneously if needed.
